title: Bitcoin (BTC) miner Riot Platforms in AI infrastructure deal with AMD
text: Riot Platforms surged 11% on news of a major lease deal with AMD marking a significant pivot toward AI infrastructure operations. The company announced an initial 25 MW data center lease agreement with AMD at the Rockdale site, with a 10-year term projected to generate $311 million in contract revenue. AMD holds expansion rights for an additional 75 MW and a right of first refusal for another 100 MW, demonstrating strong customer confidence in Riot's infrastructure capabilities. The deal represents a strategic shift for the company from pure bitcoin mining to infrastructure-as-a-service positioning targeting artificial intelligence and high-performance computing workloads. Riot's acquisition of 200 acres of land in Milam County, Texas for $96 million provided the foundation for the data center expansion. The Rockdale site features a 700 MW power connection to the grid plus dedicated water and fiber connectivity. The transaction valued bitcoin mining company's assets at premium multiples for infrastructure assets, reflecting market recognition of Riot's transition to AI infrastructure operator status.
